## Results — treatment effect by outcome

Each outcome's ITT effect $\tau$ is estimated jointly (positive = the intervention
raised that outcome). The forest is the single most communicative summary; the
pairwise contrast heatmap shows $P(\tau_k > \tau_j)$ for every outcome pair.

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary = _csv("tau_summary.csv")
tau_summary
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("tau_forest.png", "Treatment effect by outcome (forest, reference line at 0)"))
```

### Pairwise contrasts — P(τ_k > τ_j)

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("contrast_heatmap.png", "Pairwise contrast heatmap"))
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
_csv("tau_contrast_matrix.csv", index_col=0)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Taught-vs-not-taught generalisation contrast (LRPITT15/15b), when present.
_diff = _csv("tau_difference.csv")
if _diff is not None:
    _d = _diff.iloc[0]
    print("### Generalisation contrast\n")
    print(
        f"`{_d.contrast}`: difference in logit-scale effect "
        f"{_d.diff_logit_mean:+.3f} (95% CrI {_d.diff_logit_lo:+.3f} to "
        f"{_d.diff_logit_hi:+.3f}); P(difference > 0) = {_d.prob_diff_pos:.3f}. "
        "A positive difference means the directly-taught words moved more than the "
        "not-taught comparison words (limited generalisation)."
    )
```

::: {.callout-note title="ITT-vs-joint consistency"}

The single-outcome LRPITT models and this joint model estimate the *same* ITT
effects; the joint τ per outcome should track the singles. The cross-model
ITT-vs-joint forest is produced by `scripts/compare_statistical_models.py`
(`output/statistical_models/comparison/`).

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Shown here if the comparison forest has been copied alongside this report.
print(_img("itt_vs_joint_forest.png", "ITT vs joint τ consistency"))
```

:::
